title: Cyclosorus cyrtocaulos
taxonomy: Cyclosorus cyrtocaulos is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a part of the order Polypodiales. The family Thelypteridaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or the thelypterid ferns. It is closely related to the family Dryopteridaceae, which also includes various types of ferns. Cyclosorus cyrtocaulos is further classified within the genus Cyclosorus, which includes several species of ferns. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other fern species.

side_effects: Cyclosorus cyrtocaulos is a type of fern, and as with many plants, it may cause some side effects when ingested. intro: Cyclosorus cyrtocaulos, also known as the Japanese chain fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate pain and swelling, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus cyrtocaulos include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
